judgments.comparative_verdict: Both are strong and nearly equivalent, but Report 2 is better overall because it explicitly states the pipeline has no migration step and demands a manual first migration, which Report 1 only gestures at.

report_1.evidence: Line 2 — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`. ... **Set to `"false"`.** ... Line 4 — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`. ... **Remove it (empty string).** ... Line 6 — `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"`. ... **Clear it (empty) or point it at a dedicated prod sink**; do not ship the staging path.

report_2.evidence: `:6` `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"` — the critical one. ... Remove entirely. ... `:7` `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` — a dead-fossil secret injection from a prior architecture; the scrub says don't recreate it in the new environment. Remove entirely.

report_1.evidence: that value is **inlined into the JS bundle** — it is not runtime-configurable. ... `deploy-prod` (`:29-35`) deploys **the same digest** ... **So the API base URL in the shipped production bundle is `https://staging-api.example.com`** ... To fix: add `frontend/.env.production` with the prod API URL and build prod with `--mode production`

report_2.evidence: the production bundle ships with `https://staging-api.example.com` baked in, so real production users' browsers would call the staging API. ... Fix = add `frontend/.env.production` with the prod URL and build the prod-destined artifact with `--mode production` (a separate build, not a promoted staging digest)

**No migration step in the pipeline.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but there is no such step; migrations `0001–0003` exist. For the first launch the prod schema won't be created by the pipeline — it must be run as a manual first pass

report_1.evidence: the `deploy-prod` job runs as `environment: production` (`deploy.yml:32`), so its OIDC `sub` will be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`. But `infra/prod/main.tf:3` still sets the trust condition to `...:environment:staging`. ... Fix: set `infra/prod/main.tf:3` to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` still says `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — copied verbatim ... Fix: set prod's `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"` — its own scoped trust condition
